decks.notes: The deck focuses on controlling the game by countering opponent's spells, removing threats, and drawing cards. It utilizes a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw to establish dominance on the battlefield. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent by maintaining a strong hand and board presence while disrupting their game plan. Key cards like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Narset, Parter of Veils provide additional card advantage and control tools. The deck's win condition is to overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats while keeping them on the back foot with counterspells and removal.

This deck focuses on using a combination of spells and creatures to apply constant pressure on the opponent, quickly reducing their life total. Utilizing low-cost spells like Boomerang Basics and Combustion Technique, as well as efficient creatures like Gran-Gran and Stormchaser's Talent, the deck aims to establish a fast clock and maintain control of the board. By efficiently managing resources and applying consistent pressure, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and secure a victory before they can stabilize.

Utilize a combination of creatures and spells to efficiently ramp up mana and protect your key combo pieces, Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II. The deck focuses on generating value through ETB triggers, while also disrupting your opponent's game plan with hand disruption and removal spells. By assembling the Greasefang-Parhelion combo, you can overwhelm your opponent with powerful flyers and token creatures, ultimately leading to victory. The inclusion of various lands and alternate win conditions provides flexibility and resilience in different matchups, allowing for adaptability in Pioneer 1 vs 1 games.

This deck revolves around assembling the combo of Greasefang and Parhelion II to generate a powerful synergy and overwhelm the opponent. It includes cards that support the combo such as Fleeting Spirit and Monument to Endurance, while also providing disruption and removal with Fatal Push and Thoughtseize. The mana base is carefully constructed to support the combo pieces and ensure consistency in casting spells. Overall, the deck aims to quickly set up its combo to take control of the game and secure victory.

This deck focuses on utilizing cheap spells and card draw to quickly fill the graveyard with spells in order to bring back Arclight Phoenix. The deck includes spells like Opt, Consider, and Lightning Axe to fill the graveyard, as well as Treasure Cruise to draw additional cards. Additionally, the deck features a mix of burn spells and disruptive cards to keep the opponent on their toes. The goal is to bring back multiple Arclight Phoenixes early in the game and overwhelm the opponent with repeated attacks and burn spells.

This deck focuses on casting multiple spells in a turn to trigger the Arclight Phoenix ability and bring them back from the graveyard. The deck includes various cheap spells and card draw effects to help cycle through the deck quickly and get Phoenixes back onto the battlefield. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with fast, aggressive plays and capitalize on the recurring threat of the Phoenixes. This deck aims to apply constant pressure and outpace the opponent with efficient spellcasting and graveyard recursion.

The main concept of this Mono Black Aggro deck is to apply early pressure on the opponent with efficient creatures like Gifted Aetherborn and Graveyard Trespasser, while disrupting their hand with cards like Thoughtseize and Duress. The deck aims to control the board with removal spells like Fatal Push and Go for the Throat, while providing card advantage with Scheming Silvertongue and Castle Locthwain. The deck also includes some large threats like Archfiend of the Dross to close out the game quickly. Overall, the deck focuses on aggressive plays and disruption to take down the opponent.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly deal damage to the opponent using low-cost, efficient burn spells and aggressive creatures. The deck focuses on maintaining a fast pace and overwhelming the opponent with a constant stream of damage. Cards like Monastery Swiftspear, Soul-Scar Mage, and Emberheart Challenger provide early aggression, while cards like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage help remove any blockers or threats. The deck also utilizes lands like Ramunap Ruins and Mutavault for additional reach and damage. Overall, the deck aims to win the game quickly through aggressive, direct damage.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy using a combination of efficient creatures and powerful support cards. By playing creatures like Resplendent Angel and Righteous Valkyrie alongside cards like Bishop of Wings and Collected Company, the deck aims to quickly build a threatening board presence and pressure the opponent. Additionally, cards like Hushwood Verge and Multiversal Passage provide mana fixing and ramp, allowing the deck to consistently play its powerful threats ahead of schedule.

The deck focuses on using low-cost, efficient spells and creatures to deal damage quickly to the opponent. It utilizes a mix of burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, along with prowess creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage to build pressure on the opponent. The deck aims to maintain tempo by efficiently removing threats with spells like Academic Dispute and Boomerang Basics while pushing damage through with creatures like Emberheart Challenger. Overall, the deck aims to present a fast, aggressive game plan that overwhelms the opponent before they can stabilize.

The deck focuses on being a fast and aggressive Dimir deck that utilizes evasive creatures like Faerie Miscreant and Moon-Circuit Hacker to quickly chip away at the opponent's life total. It also includes disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the opponent's board while maintaining pressure. Mutavault and Drowner Floodpits provide additional creature threats, while Multiversal Passage allows for mana fixing. The inclusion of cards like Anoint with Affliction and Go for the Throat ensures the deck can handle a variety of threats, making it a versatile and potent aggro deck in Pioneer 1 vs 1 matches.

The deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo, utilizing cards like Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to overwhelm the opponent. It includes cards for protection, card draw, and mana fixing to ensure the combo pieces come together smoothly. The strategy revolves around creating a powerful board state that the opponent struggles to deal with, ultimately leading to victory through overwhelming force. The deck aims to quickly assemble the combo pieces while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Thoughtseize and Witherbloom Command.

The main concept of this Selesnya Aggro deck is to efficiently build a board presence with cheap creatures like Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic, while disrupting the opponent's game plan with Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Archon of Emeria. The deck also focuses on utilizing powerful creatures like Skyclave Apparition and Elspeth, Storm Slayer to apply pressure and control the battlefield. Collected Company gives the deck a chance to find additional threats quickly. The lands in the deck provide the necessary mana fixing to ensure a smooth curve of creatures and spells.

This deck focuses on quick and aggressive plays using low-cost creatures to overwhelm the opponent. Cards like Adeline, Resplendent Cathar and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben provide strong early-game threats, while Mox Amber and Coppercoat Vanguard help maintain momentum. The inclusion of cards like Zack Fair and Basri, Tomorrow's Champion offer additional support and buffs for your creatures. The deck also includes cards like Eiganjo, Seat of the Empire and Cavern of Souls to protect your key creatures and ensure they hit the battlefield smoothly.

The deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy using low-cost creatures and pump effects. By playing multiple creatures quickly and buffing them with cards like Thalia's Lieutenant, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent and close out the game before they can stabilize. Cards like Portable Hole and Declaration in Stone provide removal options while Abandoned Air Temple and Mutavault offer additional flexibility and reach. The deck aims to win through aggression and speed, utilizing a weenie white strategy to overrun the opponent.

This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures like Faerie Miscreant and Moon-Circuit Hacker, supported by removal spells like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Mutavault provides additional creature threats while Darkslick Shores and Watery Grave ensure consistent mana. The inclusion of powerful creatures like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse, as well as card draw from Enduring Curiosity, gives the deck a strong late-game presence to finish off the opponent.
